title: cyanea lobata subsp. baldwinii
intro: Cyanea lobata subsp. baldwinii, also known as the Hawaiian lobelia, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the island of Hawaii. The plant has been used in traditional Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antiviral and antibacterial activities. In herbal preparations, Cyanea lobata subsp. baldwinii is often used in teas, infusions, and salves, although its use is generally restricted due to its endangered status and potential toxicity.
